dominant_risk_themes: Workload Identity disabled with excessive IAM
executive_summary.narrative: This release shows a confirmed cross-domain path from disabled Workload Identity and excessive project IAM through to live runtime token access, plus a separate, unrelated pair of CVEs in the same pinned cryptography package.

cross_domain_correlations.title: Workload Identity Disabled + Excessive IAM + Live Token Access
cross_domain_correlations.description: Workload Identity is disabled (infrastructure) and a project-level Owner role is granted (infrastructure) to the service account those nodes inherit. KubeArmor independently confirms, at runtime, a pod actually accessing the GCP metadata server's service-account token endpoint.
cross_domain_correlations.business_impact: A compromised pod can obtain a token with project-level access via the metadata server — confirmed reachable, not theoretical.
cross_domain_correlations.recommended_action: Enable Workload Identity and remove the Owner role together — the runtime finding confirms this path is real, not hypothetical.

cross_domain_correlations.title: Cryptography Package — Two CVEs, One Upgrade
cross_domain_correlations.description: The same pinned cryptography package version has two independently-disclosed CVEs reported separately by Snyk.
cross_domain_correlations.business_impact: Both vulnerabilities are present simultaneously in every backend instance running this pinned version.
cross_domain_correlations.recommended_action: Upgrade cryptography past both CVEs in a single dependency bump.

top_risks.title: Confirmed path from disabled Workload Identity to live metadata-server token access
top_risks.impact: Live, confirmed runtime evidence of an infrastructure misconfiguration being actively reachable.
top_risks.why_it_matters: This is not a static finding — KubeArmor saw it happen.

priority_actions.title: Enable Workload Identity and scope IAM together
priority_actions.rationale: Addresses the highest-confidence finding in this release.

priority_actions.title: Upgrade cryptography
priority_actions.rationale: One dependency bump resolves two CVEs.

release_readiness.rationale: A confirmed, live cross-domain privilege-escalation path is independently sufficient to block this release.
